text: The surgeon clamps the renal artery to halt blood flow to the kidney, which has been rotated 180 degrees for resection. Using cold scissors, the surgeon begins cutting around the exophytic tumor, revealing its capsule. The tumor is gently separated from the kidney, with visible arteries entering the tumor. Despite its large size, the tumor peels away easily due to its outward growth from the kidney.

The surgeon completes the resection of the kidney tumor, revealing a thick capsule filled with old hemorrhage. The tumor is removed, and the surgeon fulgurates the base to ensure hemostasis and destroy any residual tumor tissue. The kidney repair involves placing plastic bolsters to support sutures that bring the edges of the kidney together, minimizing the defect and stopping bleeding.

The surgeon prepares to clamp the renal artery, which is visible in front of a large central mass. The spleen is seen in the background. The CT scan is used to evaluate the tumor's location, which is adjacent to the renal artery and centrally located within the kidney. The surgeon clamps the artery and begins cutting into the kidney capsule, revealing the tumor capsule beneath. The proximity of the tumor to the renal vessels is noted, and an arterial bleed is observed during the incision.

The surgeon continues to dissect the tumor from the kidney, identifying and clipping a small artery connected to the tumor. The tumor, encapsulated and round, peels away easily from the normal kidney tissue beneath it. The tumor is removed and placed into a bag to prevent spillage. A hemostatic bolster, Surgicel, is sutured in place to apply pressure on the repair site, ensuring hemostasis due to the broad defect near the artery.

The surgeon addresses a small superior mass on the top of the kidney, measuring approximately 1.5 centimeters. The tumor is resected without clamping the renal artery, allowing continuous blood flow to the kidney. Minimal bleeding occurs during the procedure. Two sutures are used to repair the small defect, and Surgicel is applied to the resection site to minimize post-operative bleeding. The surrounding fat is repositioned over the kidney at the end of the procedure.
